title: crepis montana
intro: Crepis montana, also known as the rough hawk's beard, is a perennial plant species belonging to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive issues and skin irritations. The plant's roots and leaves have been used to create herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which may provide health benefits such as reducing fever, improving digestion, and promoting wound healing. Additionally, Crepis montana has been traditionally used to treat various conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions like eczema and acne.
